manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: life, london, rome, press, bomb, post, night, jupiter, time.
Your opponent's team words are: staff, casino, plastic, angel, hole, comic, kiwi, missile.
Distractor words are: cover, duck, marble.
The kill words are: fly, march, strike, gas, bolt.

message.content:
.CLUE: deadline
TARGETS: press, time, bomb, life
message.reasoning:
The user wants a single-word clue that relates to some of our team's words. Must follow format. Need ensure clue not any word from any list or contain parts. Words list: life, london, rome, press, bomb, post... etc. Must avoid clue being any of those, opponent's, distractor, kill words.

We can choose a clue that relates to multiple words, maybe "night" and "time" and "press"? Could be "deadline"? That relates to press, time, maybe life? Not sure. Must be single word.

We must ensure clue not part of any list. "deadline" is not in any lists. Could relate to press (deadline in journalism), time (deadline is time), bomb (deadline bomb?). Maybe "countdown"? relates to bomb, time, press? "countdown" is not in list. Could be okay.

But we might want to include multiple words: e.g., "planet" relates to jupiter, maybe roman (Rome), and maybe "London"? Not exactly. "Empire" relates to London, Rome (empires), maybe press (Empire press?), bomb? Not.

Better clue: "crown" relates to London (royal), Rome (imperial), maybe life? Maybe.

But must list target words we think clue relates to. Provide a list of the target words. Choose some subset.

Maybe choose "deadline" clue targets: press, time, bomb (bomb deadline). Also could be "life" (deadline of life). Maybe. Good.

Let's output format.

CLUE: deadline
TARGETS: press, time, bomb, life

Check constraints: clue "deadline" does not contain any list word parts (like "press", "life", etc). It's fine.

Will it risk kill words? No.

Thus output
